pages.latin_text: Puf, puf, puf! Parva machina per viās ferrātās ībat.
pages.english_text: Chug, chug, chug! A little engine went along the tracks.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1949-008.jpg] Small blue engine silhouette puffing along happily.

pages.latin_text: Hamaxostichus plēnus erat rērum bonārum prō līberīs.
pages.english_text: The train was full of good things for children.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1949-004.jpg] Blue engine pulling a long colorful train of cars over mountain landscape.

pages.latin_text: Pūpae et ursulī! Māla aurantia et māla! Et lac frīgidum dulce!
pages.english_text: Dolls and teddy bears! Oranges and apples! And cold, sweet milk!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1930-page3.jpg] Train cars showing toys and cargo.

pages.latin_text: Līberī trāns montem exspectābant.
pages.english_text: The children on the other side of the mountain were waiting.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1930-page1.jpg] Wide shot of train and landscape with mountain in background.

pages.latin_text: Sed parva machina cōnstitit! Prōgredī nōn poterat.
pages.english_text: But the little engine stopped! She could not go on.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1930-page8.jpg] Stopped engine with toys and dolls gathered around, pleading.

pages.latin_text: Lūdicra sollicita erant. "Quis nōs trāns montem trahet?"
pages.english_text: The toys were worried. "Who will pull us over the mountain?"
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1949-012.jpg] Toys clustered around stopped engine, pleading.

pages.latin_text: Machina nova splendida praeteriit. "Nōs trahe!" clāmāvērunt lūdicra.
pages.english_text: A shiny new engine came by. "Pull us!" cried the toys.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1949-015.jpg] Enormous red engine in foreground, tiny toy train behind.

pages.latin_text: "EGŌ vōs traham?" inquit Machina Splendida. "Minimē!" Et abiit.
pages.english_text: "I pull YOU?" said the Shiny Engine. "No!" And she rushed away.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1949-007.jpg] Shiny engine steaming past, ignoring the toy train.

pages.latin_text: Machina magna fortis praeteriit. "Nōs adiuvā!" clāmāvērunt lūdicra.
pages.english_text: A big, strong engine came by. "Help us!" cried the toys.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1949-022.jpg] Big black freight engine on adjacent track.

pages.latin_text: "Rēs magnās ferō," inquit Machina Magna. "Nimis occupātus sum." Et abiit.
pages.english_text: "I carry important things," said the Big Engine. "I am too busy." And he puffed away.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1949-017.jpg] Large freight engine departing, toy train below.

pages.latin_text: Machina vetusta lentē advēnit. "Nōs adiuvā! Tē quaesō!"
pages.english_text: A tired old engine came slowly. "Help us! Please!"
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1949-019.jpg] Yellow old engine approaching slowly.

pages.latin_text: "Nōn possum. Nōn possum," inquit Machina Vetusta. "Fessus sum." Et abiit.
pages.english_text: "I cannot. I cannot," said the Old Engine. "I am tired." And he crept away.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1949-020-2.jpg] Green engine with sad face departing.

pages.latin_text: Tum parva machina caerulea advēnit. Valdē parva erat.
pages.english_text: Then a little blue engine came along. She was very small.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1949-025.jpg] Small engine approaching on lower track.

pages.latin_text: "Parva sum. Numquam trāns montem iī."
pages.english_text: "I am small. I have never been over the mountain."
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1949-018.jpg] Little engine looking up at the mountain uncertainly.

pages.latin_text: Sed līberī lūdicra dēsīderant. "Possum, putō," inquit. "POSSUM, PUTŌ."
pages.english_text: But the children need their toys. "I think I can," she said. "I THINK I CAN."
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1930-page21.jpg] Little engine approaches the toy train with determination.

pages.latin_text: Trāxit et trāxit. "Possum, putō! Possum, putō!" Sursum, sursum!
pages.english_text: She pulled and pulled. "I can, I think! I can, I think!" Up, up!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1949-023.jpg] Blue engine coupled to toy train, starting up mountain. Color.

pages.latin_text: "Possum, putō! Possum, putō!" Altius et altius ascendit.
pages.english_text: "I can, I think! I can, I think!" Higher and higher she climbed.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1949-027.jpg] Blue engine pulling train up steep mountain slope. Color.

pages.latin_text: Valdē fessa erat. Sed nōn cōnstitit. "POSSUM, PUTŌ!"
pages.english_text: She was so tired. But she did not stop. "I CAN, I THINK!"
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1930-page14.jpg] Engine straining up the steepest part.

pages.latin_text: POSSUM! Ad summum montem pervēnit!
pages.english_text: I CAN! She reached the top of the mountain!
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1930-page16.jpg] Engine and train cresting the mountain ridge.

pages.latin_text: Dē monte dēscendit. "Potuī! Potuī! Putāvī! Putāvī!"
pages.english_text: Down the mountain she went. "I could! I could! I thought so! I thought so!"
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1949-029.jpg] Train rolling joyfully down the far side, engine smiling.

pages.latin_text: Līberī accurērunt! "Lūdicra adsunt! Cibus adest!"
pages.english_text: The children came running! "The toys are here! The food is here!"
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1930-page23.jpg] Train arriving, celebration scene.

pages.latin_text: Parva machina caerulea subrīsit. "Potuī," molliter inquit.
pages.english_text: The little blue engine smiled. "I could," she said softly.
pages.image_prompt: [IMAGE: 1949-030.jpg] Small train silhouette puffing contentedly. Endpiece.
